app.name: foxguard
app.one_liner: A security scanner as fast as a linter, written in Rust.

screens.name: TUI Findings View
screens.purpose: Interactive terminal interface for scanning, diffing, and reviewing security findings with in-app triage actions.
primary_actions: Add to baseline or ignore rules

rust_commands.name: foxguard_diff
rust_commands.purpose: Show new findings compared to a target branch.

rust_commands.name: foxguard_scan
rust_commands.purpose: Perform fast static analysis scan with taint tracking across supported languages.

rust_commands.name: foxguard_tui
rust_commands.purpose: Launch interactive terminal UI for local security triage with scan, diff, and secrets modes.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build TUI scaffold with mode picker (Scan/Diff/Secrets)
week 1: Expose CNSA 2.0 and crypto metadata in findings output
week 1: Implement core taint engine with per-sanitizer-group AST traversal
week 1: Integrate Semgrep-compatible YAML rule loader
week 2: Add in-app triage actions (review, ignore, baseline)
week 2: Implement GitHub PR inline comment posting
week 2: Surface compliance metadata in TUI detail pane
week 2: Update README to reflect TUI, PQ, and CBOM features
acceptance_tests: `foxguard diff main` correctly identifies and displays only new findings versus the main branch.
CNSA 2.0 and post-quantum crypto metadata are visible in the TUI detail pane for relevant findings.
Findings marked as 'ignored' persist in configuration and do not reappear on subsequent scans.
GitHub PR integration posts findings as inline review comments when `--github-pr` flag is used.
User can launch `foxguard tui .` and select Scan mode to view findings with dataflow paths within 0.5 seconds on a 10k LOC repo.
